findings.title: Gap evidence arrays are not validated for emptiness
findings.why_it_matters: Gaps with empty evidence arrays produce decorative analysis that passes schema validation but provides no actionable signal to reviewers or downstream consumers.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a validation step in the gap detection stage that rejects or flags any gap entry whose evidence array is empty.
findings.downstream_risk: Working paper includes unsupported gap claims that survive schema validation, misleading regulatory reviewers.

findings.title: Readiness scorer does not penalise gaps with missing source references
findings.why_it_matters: The readiness score may be artificially high when gap analysis is present but untraced, giving a false signal that the working paper is ready to proceed.
findings.recommended_fix: Weight readiness score down proportionally to the fraction of gaps lacking evidence references.
findings.downstream_risk: Working paper proceeds to draft stage with insufficient evidence, requiring rework downstream.

failure_scenarios.description: Transcript with only decorative section headers produces an empty gap list that passes validation, preventing reviewers from detecting missing evidence.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Add a minimum substantive content check before gap detection runs; warn when input has fewer than N sentences of technical content.

failure_scenarios.description: Two slide claims covering the same frequency band but different modulation schemes are merged into one gap, losing the distinction.
failure_scenarios.mitigation: Require conflict markers when merging claims with differing technical parameters.

minimum_bar_to_proceed: F-001 must be resolved before this subsystem is considered production-ready. F-002 should be addressed before the first regulatory submission.
